text: The surgeon performs a laparoscopic total proctocolectomy, beginning with the inferior to superior mobilization of the right colon. The peritoneum is incised along the root of the small bowel mesentery, and the right colon is mobilized until the second part of the duodenum is visualized, marked with a gauze piece. The ileocolic pedicle is tented up, incised, and dissected down to its origin, stripped of lymphatics and fibrofatty tissue, then clipped and divided. The right colon is further mobilized in a medial to lateral fashion. The middle colic vessels are dissected by incising the peritoneum along the posterior surface of the transverse mesocolon, revealing the vessels and their early division into right and left branches, marked with gauze for identification.

The surgeon incises the peritoneum along the line of torque to take down the lateral attachments of the right colon. Using a harmonic scalpel, the gastrocolic omentum is divided, fully mobilizing the colon from the stomach. Dissection continues along the root of the transverse colon, revealing the posterior dissection window. The hepatic flexure is completely mobilized by taking down its peritoneal attachments. The anterior portion of the middle colic vessels is dissected, with the previously placed gauze piece aiding in identification. The pedicles are clipped and divided, and the gauze piece is removed.

The surgeon begins rectal mobilization by dissecting the inferior mesenteric vein adjacent to the DJ flexor, elevating it and developing a posterior plane. The vein is dissected, clipped, and divided. Rectal mobilization continues at the sacral promontory by incising the right leaf of the rectum mesentery just below the inferior mesenteric artery. The pedicle is dissected down to its origin from the aorta, cleared of fibro fatty tissue, clipped, and divided. The left colon is mobilized medially to laterally, with lateral attachments taken down. The peritoneal fold is removed with medial traction, and the splenic flexure is completely mobilized using monopolar cautery.

The surgeon divides the left portion of the transverse mesocolon along the inferior border of the pancreas, completing the immobilization of the abdominal colon. Rectal dissection begins along the right leaf of the rectal knee sentry, with the peritoneal incision extending to the pelvic floor. The right hypogastric nerves are visible and preserved as dissection continues medial to them in the avascular presacral plane, mobilizing the rectum posteriorly to the pelvic floor. A prominent middle rectum is clipped and divided. The anterior peritoneal fold is incised, and anterior dissection is performed posterior to Denonvilliers fascia, mobilizing the rectum on the seminal vesicles. The dissection proceeds around the right lateral side, connecting anterior and posterior planes to mobilize the rectum on the right side, and similarly on the left side, circumferentially mobilizing the rectum until the levator muscles are visible.

The surgeon completes the circumferential mobilization of the rectum down to the anorectal junction. A lone star retractor is applied to evert the anal canal, which is infiltrated with a dilute adrenaline solution. The mucosa is incised along the dentate line, and a complete anal mucosectomy is performed. The mucosal tube is sewn shut, and a gauze piece is placed in the anal canal. The rectum is divided at the anorectal junction, and the gauze piece is removed to expose the mucosal tube. With traction, the muscular core of the rectum is visible, forming the internal sphincter. The anorectal junction is completely transected to free the specimen.

The surgical specimen is exteriorized through an Alexis wound port. A standard J pouch is fashioned using a linear stapler, with redundant mesentery incised and mesenteric peritoneum scored to ensure adequate pouch reach. The pouch is deposited into the abdomen and delivered into the anal canal using a proline suture at its apex. The apex of the pouch is opened, and an interrupted hand-sewn ileal pouch anastomosis is performed with 3-0 Vicryl sutures to complete the procedure.
